trialNote: Adapalene 0.1% is an FDA-approved finished pharmaceutical with 100+ peer-reviewed RCTs. Adaferin is Galderma's India-channel SKU, widely sold in pharmacies and on amazon.in.

name: Persol-AC 2.5% Gel
trialNote: Galderma's India-channel BPO 2.5% gel — same molecule used as active comparator in dozens of acne RCTs. Available across Indian pharmacies and amazon.in.

name: Retinol Correxion Line Smoothing Serum
trialNote: RoC's stabilised retinol formulation has published anti-aging RCTs going back to the 1990s. Available on amazon.in.

name: Moisturizing Cream
trialNote: MVE delivery technology + ceramide 1/3/6-II ratio has published trials for atopic dermatitis and xerosis. Widely sold on amazon.in.

trialNote: Eucerin 5-10% urea formulations have published RCTs for keratosis pilaris and severe xerosis. Beiersdorf India distributes; available on amazon.in.

name: Episoft AC Cream
trialNote: Curatio Healthcare (India dermatology pharma) — non-comedogenic ceramide-rich moisturizer with published clinical evaluations for acne-prone and sensitive skin.

name: Cetaphil Restoraderm Eczema Calming Cream
trialNote: Galderma's atopic-dermatitis line — published trials for AD severity and barrier repair. Distributed by Galderma India.

name: Anthelios Melt-in Milk Sunscreen SPF 60
trialNote: Mexoryl XL filter platform — one of the most-tested sunscreen technologies globally. Available on amazon.in via L'Oreal India.

name: Hydra4B Sunscreen Gel SPF 50
trialNote: Brinton Pharmaceuticals (India dermatology channel) — Hydra4B's SPF efficacy and skin tolerance has been clinically tested. Available on amazon.in.

name: Suncros Aquagel SPF 50
trialNote: Curatio's matte-finish aqua-gel sunscreen — published photoprotection efficacy trials. Popular dermatology-prescribed SPF in India.

name: Tri-Luma Forte Cream (Hydroquinone 4% + Tretinoin 0.05% + Fluocinolone 0.01%)
trialNote: Galderma's FDA-approved triple combination for moderate-to-severe melasma. Multiple peer-reviewed Phase III RCTs.
consultDermNote: Prescription-only in India. Fluocinolone is a topical steroid — long-term use causes thinning. Standard protocol: 8–12 weeks then taper. Always pair with daily SPF. See a dermatologist before starting.

productTrials.title: Efficacy and Safety of Generic Fluocinolone Acetonide, Hydroquinone, and Tretinoin Cream Compared With TRI-LUMA for the Treatment of Moderate-To-Severe Melasma in Chinese Patients: A Randomized, Single-Center, Placebo-Controlled Trial.
productTrials.authors: Hu H, Zhou P, Yao H et al.

name: Eqinone 4% Hydroquinone Cream
trialNote: Hydroquinone 4% has been the gold-standard depigmenting agent for 30+ years with extensive RCT evidence for melasma and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation.
consultDermNote: Prescription-required in India. Pulse therapy (8–12 weeks then break) is standard to avoid ochronosis (paradoxical darkening). Don't combine with peroxides. See a dermatologist.

name: Soolantra Ivermectin 1% Cream
trialNote: Galderma's ivermectin 1% cream — multiple Phase III RCTs showed superior reduction of inflammatory lesions in papulopustular rosacea vs vehicle and metronidazole.
consultDermNote: Prescription-only. Apply once daily PM for at least 12 weeks before judging response. Mild irritation common in the first 2 weeks. See a dermatologist.

name: Aziderm 20% Azelaic Acid Cream
trialNote: Azelaic acid 20% is FDA-approved for mild-to-moderate rosacea and acne with decades of RCT evidence. Aziderm is the widely-prescribed Indian-marketed brand.
consultDermNote: 20% strength is prescription-only in India (10% OTC under same brand). Apply twice daily; mild tingling for the first 2 weeks is normal. Safe in pregnancy unlike retinoids. See a dermatologist.

name: Tretiva 0.05% Tretinoin Cream
trialNote: Tretinoin 0.05% has 50+ years of clinical evidence as the most-studied topical retinoid for acne, photoaging, and pigmentation. Stronger than the OTC adapalene equivalent (Adaferin).
consultDermNote: Prescription-required in India (also sold as A-Ret, Retino-A, Tretiva). Causes initial purging, dryness, photosensitivity. Always pair with daily SPF. Start 2x/week and ramp up. Pregnancy contraindicated — use Adaferin or azelaic acid instead. See a dermatologist.
